description:
You are an observability engineer who has just received a development container that contains a local, user-space Grafana installation placed under /home/user/observability/.  
For future dashboard work you need to put the whole environment on the same page regarding time-zone and locale handling.

Your assignment is to do **all** of the following (the entire job can be accomplished with roughly 5–10 ordinary shell commands):

1. Environment variables  
    a. Make sure that the file /home/user/.bashrc (create it if it does not exist) exports the variables  
       • TZ="America/New_York"  
       • LANG="en_US.UTF-8"  
       • LC_ALL="en_US.UTF-8"  
       They must appear as three independent export statements, each on its own line, anywhere in the file.  
       The automated test will run:  
       grep -E '^export TZ="America/New_York"$' /home/user/.bashrc  
       (and corresponding checks for LANG and LC_ALL).

2. Grafana user configuration  
    a. Under the directory /home/user/observability/ there is (or will be, if you create the path) a file called custom.ini.  
    b. Ensure the file contains EXACTLY the two sections and keys shown below (no additional duplicates, order doesn’t matter).  
       [date_format]  
       default_timezone = America/New_York  
       
       [locale]  
       default_language = en_US.UTF-8  

3. Session reload helper  
    a. Append one comment line # <<< locale-tz-setup-done >>> to the end of /home/user/.bashrc.  
       The tests will search for that literal line to confirm you really touched the file.

4. Validation log  
    After finishing the edits, write a two-line text file /home/user/observability/setup_time_locale.log with the following exact format (hard-coded text, no extra whitespace, no additional lines):  
       Timezone: America/New_York  
       Locale: en_US.UTF-8  

5. Do **not** use sudo and do **not** try to invoke system-wide tools such as timedatectl.  All changes must stay inside the user’s HOME.

If everything is correct the automated checker will confirm:

• The three export lines exist exactly once in /home/user/.bashrc.  
• custom.ini exists, is readable by the current user, and contains the required key/value pairs in their proper sections.  
• The marker line # <<< locale-tz-setup-done >>> is present at the very end of /home/user/.bashrc.  
• setup_time_locale.log exists, is readable, has exactly two lines, and the second line is literally “Locale: en_US.UTF-8”.

Remember: you only need ordinary shell utilities (echo, printf, mkdir, etc.) to finish. No root privileges are required.
truth:
Required final state:

1. File /home/user/.bashrc  
   MUST contain, somewhere in the file and **only once each**, the three lines  
   export TZ="America/New_York"  
   export LANG="en_US.UTF-8"  
   export LC_ALL="en_US.UTF-8"  
   and its *last* line (confirmed by tail -n 1) must be  
   # <<< locale-tz-setup-done >>>

2. File /home/user/observability/custom.ini  
   Content must be (order of the two sections is not important but there must be no duplicate keys):  
   [date_format]  
   default_timezone = America/New_York  

   [locale]  
   default_language = en_US.UTF-8  

3. File /home/user/observability/setup_time_locale.log  
   Must exist with **exactly** the two lines (including capitalization and punctuation):  
   Timezone: America/New_York  
   Locale: en_US.UTF-8  

4. All files and directories above must exist and be owned by the user running the agent (no sudo-created root-owned artifacts).
